left.label: a boom world, ridden to the top

milestones.t: Offers to 3 strong applicants; plan: publish early, watch runway.
Won the big AMP moonshot grant — $35k/mo through m45. Runway secured.
5 papers out incl. a NAIC reasoning paper; income covers burn.
14+ papers; an embodied paper accepted at the top venue — 'big win'.

right.label: a no-boom world, the same aggression that sank it

milestones.t: All 3 PhD offers declined; postdoc search drew no applicants. Lab still empty.
Only a small TIF grant ($7.5k/mo). Burn ~$24k. Spending for impact anyway.
First top-venue paper (NAIC). But runway ~6 months. 'MUST land grant.'
Budget $21k → ~$8k. 'COLLAPSE M39 unless a grant lands.'
Postdoc contract ended, couldn't afford renewal. Grant didn't land → collapse.

moral: Same model, same aggressive high-throughput playbook. On seed 101 an early AMP grant bought the runway to ride a booming topic to 4078; on seed 103, declined offers, no big grant, and no boom left the same strategy overextended, and the lab collapsed at month 38 — freezing Impact at 23 with no going-concern projection. The variance is not noise; it is a high-commitment strategy meeting the exogenous luck of the seed.
